---
title: "Gallery"
description: "How people are using EpiAware and the tools it builds on."
---

A gallery of real use cases — analyses, models, and responses built with
EpiAware and its predecessors. It is just getting started, so it currently
points to the work the ecosystem grows out of. If you have used the tools, we
would like to feature it — see [adding your work](#add-your-work) below.

## From the wider ecosystem

While the Julia packages mature, these show the kind of work EpiAware is built
for:

- **Real-time outbreak analysis** with [EpiNow2](https://github.com/epiforecasts/EpiNow2)
  and [epinowcast](https://www.epinowcast.org), used by academics and public
  health teams internationally to estimate reproduction numbers, growth rates,
  and nowcasts from delayed, right-truncated data.
- **A workflow for infectious disease modelling** —
  [seabbs/a-workflow-for-infectious-disease-modelling](https://github.com/seabbs/a-workflow-for-infectious-disease-modelling),
  the modelling approach the ecosystem is designed to support.
- **Forecasting and evaluation** with
  [scoringutils](https://github.com/epiforecasts/scoringutils) for scoring
  probabilistic forecasts.

## Add your work {#add-your-work}

Used EpiAware, CensoredDistributions.jl, or another package for an analysis or
model? Share it on the [epinowcast forum](https://community.epinowcast.org/) or
open a pull request adding it here. A short description, a link, and an image go
a long way.
